snippet.title: Prices, Pensions and Politics: UK Economic Outlook: Bloomberg UK Show
snippet.description:
The UK's Conservative Party is under electoral pressure over the cost of living crisis, poor economic growth and a raft of corporate criticism. One of Chancellor Jeremy Hunt's key responses has been the Mansion House Compact, an agreement for nine major pension providers to boost their investment in growth companies. We speak to one of the signatories, Phoenix Group CEO Andy Briggs. "Bloomberg UK" focuses on the biggest economic, financial and political issues facing Britain. Hosted by Francine Lacqua, the program goes beyond the market moves with expert analysis and top interviews from the heart of London.


 --------
Follow Bloomberg for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more: http://www.bloomberg.com

snippet.title: Andrew Briggs - Events in Quantum Mechanics and Relativity
snippet.description:
Get free access to Closer To Truth's library of 5,000 videos: https://closertotruth.com/

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Quantum mechanics, the best theory of the very small, and general relativity, the best theory of the very large, are deeply incompatible. One way to compare and contrast the two is by discerning the nature of an “event” in each. It is very different.

Support the show with Closer To Truth merchandise: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Watch more interviews on quantum theory: https://bit.ly/3Q2vx5c

Andrew Briggs is Professor of Nanomaterials at the University of Oxford. He is Fellow of St Anne’s College, Fellow of Wolfson College, Fellow of the Institute of Physics, Honorary Fellow of the Royal Microscopical Society, and Member of International Society for Science and Religion and Academia Europaea.

Register for free at CTT.com for subscriber-only exclusives: https://bit.ly/3He94Ns

Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. Engage new and diverse ways of thinking. Appreciate intense debates. Share your own opinions. Seek your own answers.

snippet.title: Welcome to Gigg Lane - Andy Briggs | Incoming | Bury FC
snippet.description:
Bury FC are delighted to confirm Andy Briggs has joined the club ahead of the 2023/24 season.

The 25-year-old striker joins the club from NWCFL Division One North side Steeton where he scored 35 goals last season.

Briggs has played in his home West Yorkshire town since the age of 16 and has spent the last nine seasons playing in Steeton green, he’s played through their journey from amateur football to them joining the National League System.

Upon joining the club, he said: “I felt like it was time for a new challenge and when Andy [Welsh] called me and said he wanted me to come down here, it was almost a no-brainer.”

“Just looking at the setup down here and where this club plans to be and wants to be, it’s going to be a real great experience”

Thank you for your continued support of the club. Bury FC is a community, fan-owned football club competing in the North West Counties Football League Premier Division.  We're by the fans, for the fans, and we couldn't do this without you.

#BuryFC

https://thepenultimatecuriosity.com

snippet.description:
An Ecuadorian woman has risen from the 'dead' after almost being buried alive.

Shocking video footage shows the moment 76-year-old Bella Montoya was rescued from her coffin, after being stuck inside for over four hours.

The woman was declared deceased after suffering a stroke in hospital and taken to a local funeral home for preparations.

#BuriedAlive #Ecuador #BellaMontoya #Coffin #10NewsFirst

snippet.title: Former CIA Spy on BAD Relationships 💘💑 | #shorts #dating
snippet.description:
Ex-CIA Spy Andrew Bustamante talks about how, because of emotions, people coming out of a bad relationship often pivot and then date someone that is a total opposite from their original relationship.  He explains how this does not make logical sense, because if there were a lot of things that you liked about the person in the original relationship – you can make some fine-tune adjustments and find a better relationship without having to go to extremes.

Credit: Excerpts taken from the October 17, 2022 interview with Andrew Bustamante on the Chris Collins podcast.

#cia  #humanintelligence  #relationships  #dating  #breakups  #lifehacks  #lovelife  #datingadvice  #badrelationships  #spy  #emotions  #emotional #andrewbustamante #quotes, #decisions

snippet.description: Novak Djokovic probably would have beaten Alex de Minaur at the 2023 Australian Open in any circumstances, but he might not have humiliated the Demon at his native AO if the Aussie had bit his tongue a year earlier.
